Win a set is near-lock given Korda serving 70%+ 1stWon.

SCOUT+SIM: Hijikata +1.5 73% true vs 55c (+18pp edge). Norrie's lefty serve creates disruption risk, but Hijikata's cross-court BH pattern neutralizes the wide deuce kick — he attacks lefty serves structurally better than most. Grinder vs grinder on slow IW hard: Hijikata's lateral speed and rally tolerance means he won't be aced off court. Market at 73c Norrie is pure #27 vs #117 ranking anchor — form says coin flip. Scout adj: -8pp (lefty -3, fatigue -3, venue pedigree -2). Gate 15.5% cleared by +2.5pp. Sized 1.5u: wins even if Norrie wins the match.

SCOUT+SIM: Bencic +1.5 82% true vs 63c (+19pp edge). PLAYSTYLE: Bencic hits early, flat, and deep — she takes pace off Pegula's baseline drives before Pegula can reset position. Pegula's game depends on rallying rhythm and court-position defense; Bencic strips both. H2H 4-0 Bencic — 2021 USO 6-2 6-4, Tokyo 6-3 6-3 scorelines are the structural baseline, not flukes. Pegula's slow-start pattern documented: dropped R1 vs Vekic, dropped R1 vs Ostapenko at IW. Bencic 9-2, dropped ZERO sets at IW 2026. Night session (cooler, denser air, high-bounce Laykold) slows the court — favors Bencic's pace-through-the-ball game over Pegula's redirecting style. Only losing scenario: Bencic loses in straights — lowest-probability outcome given all data.

SCOUT+SIM: Shevchenko ML 55% true vs 37c (+18pp, gate 12% B=0.21, clears by +6pp). Full Scout+Sim convergence. PLAYSTYLE: Quinn's 64c is a seeding trap. He's 4-6 in 2026, 8-17 career ATP hard record (32%). Straight-set losses to Opelka, Cilic, Jodar (#198) — regression is real. Serve inconsistency: 7.71 aces/match but 2.14 DF/match, flat aggressive style that needs perfect timing he doesn't have right now, shanks UEs out of form. H2H irrelevant: clay, best-of-5, Roland Garros 2025. Shevchenko at 37c is the steal: 9-8 in 2026 vs Quinn's 4-6, actually hits MORE aces (8.33/match), better match rhythm, just won at IW before losing to Ruud. On outdoor hard where Quinn needs to be clicking, Shevchenko forces extra balls and extracts UEs from a player already handing them out.

SCOUT+SIM: Djokovic ML 68% true vs 59c (+9pp, gate ~8% B=0.34, clears by +1pp). OVERRIDE: Scout rec 1u, player sized 1.5u. PLAYSTYLE: IW slow court (CPI 37) is Djokovic's weapon — night conditions blunt Draper's flat pace, spin sits up into Djokovic's strike zone. Critical Scout finding: Draper posted 2ndW=41% vs Cerundolo at IW — fatal against the best returner on tour. Platform stance almost certainly load management on recovering arm, generates less kinetic chain from legs, second serve becomes sitting-duck pace. Djokovic targets this relentlessly. Draper can hit heavy, but hitting through Djokovic on slow IW requires 3-4 more shots per rally — third match back from 8 months off, arm takes cumulative punishment. Big sim/Scout divergence: base sim 40.3% Djokovic (Draper p_srv=0.704 > 0.682), Scout overrides to 68% on 2nd serve vulnerability + injury fatigue. Market priced Draper's raw first-serve without platform-stance discount.

SCOUT+SIM: Medvedev ML 68% true vs 62c (+6pp, gate ~8% B=0.37, clears by +1pp). OVERRIDE: Scout rec 1u, player sized 2.5u. PLAYSTYLE: Draper's Djokovic win is a trap — Djokovic at IW 2026 is not peak Djokovic, historically struggles with motivation in the desert. That win inflates Draper's market price artificially. The crux is Medvedev's return vs Draper's 2nd serve: Draper's platform stance (arm load management) posted 41% 2ndW vs Cerundolo at IW — a structural weakness. Medvedev stands deep in the parking lot and grinds 2nd serves with laser positioning, deliberately hunting it and forcing Draper into long baseline rallies on a vulnerable shoulder. Morning IW courts (CPI 38-39): Draper gets slightly more pace, but still high-bounce and gritty — exactly where Medvedev's defensive wall thrives. Base sim 51.6% Medvedev (raw serve profiles nearly identical); Scout override to 68% on 2nd serve vulnerability + market overreaction to Draper's Djokovic scalp. Market fair value 68-70c per Scout.

SCOUT+SIM: Mochizuki ML 55% true vs 40c (+15pp, gate 12% B=0.22, clears by +3pp). Same surface-mispricing thesis as Wong vs Burruchaga. PLAYSTYLE: Navone (#79 ATP) is another South American clay specialist — 7-24 ATP hard record (22.6%). His heavy topspin serve sits in the strike zone on hard courts. Caribbean heat at Cap Cana makes the ball fly faster, rewarding early aggressive striking — exactly Navone's weakness as a deep-returning grinder. Mochizuki is IMG Academy-developed, entire game built for hard courts, takes the ball early and aggressively. H2H played once on clay at Cagliari 2024, Mochizuki took a set off Navone on his BEST surface — flipping to hard court swings the structural advantage entirely. Caveat: Mochizuki's serve inconsistent (44-56% 1stIn variance), so Navone's defensive baseline grinding will force long rallies even when outclassed.

SCOUT+SIM: Royer ML 63% true vs 59c (+4pp Scout anchor, sim 74.9%, gate ~8% cleared). PLAYSTYLE: Pure surface pricing. Navone (#79) is 22.6% ATP hard court — same clay-specialist trap as Burruchaga/Wong. His heavy topspin serve sits in the strike zone on hard, and his return-deep-and-grind style gets outpaced when opponents can dictate tempo. Royer is 70-40 career hard court, his flat-serve game is built for outdoor Caribbean hard where the ball flies. Royer served 11 aces at 79% 1stW vs a solid returner (RBA) — that's real current form. H2H: Royer beat Navone on hard at Shanghai — the surface result already exists. Navone dropped a set to Mochizuki in R16 despite winning — not resilience, just hard-court struggle. Market anchored on Navone's seeding (#6 in this draw), not his surface record.

SCOUT+SIM: Bellucci ML 62% true vs 42c (+20pp blended Scout/sim, gate 12% cleared decisively). PLAYSTYLE: LH serve is a structural weapon on outdoor hard — wide angle to deuce court, kick to backhand — guarantees free points that Collignon cannot pattern-read away. Bellucci beat Hurkacz 6-2 6-4 in the previous round: even accounting for Hurkacz playing a 175 for fitness, those numbers don't happen by accident. He was reading the serve perfectly and executing with clean ball-striking. Market is anchoring to Collignon's long-term Challenger stats (83-51 hard) and treating the Hurkacz scalp as an anomaly — it's not. Sim 72.8% vs 42c market (+30.8pp); Scout qual conservatively 52% (+10pp). Split at 62% = +20pp, both anchors clear the 12% gate. Best value on the Challenger boards today.

SCOUT+SIM: Svitolina ML 46% true vs 29c (+17pp Scout anchor, sim 61.3%, gate ~12% B=0.69, clears by 5pp). PLAYSTYLE: Svitolina at 29c is wildly mispriced. She's 19-3 in 2026, just beat Swiatek 6-2 4-6 6-4, has 3 top-5 wins this season — market is pricing her like a journeyman. IW slow courts (CPI 37) are tailor-made for her defensive counter-punching game: she absorbs pace, redirects angles, forces opponents into rally errors. Rybakina's flat power serve gets neutralized when the court slows down the ball — Svitolina will sit behind the baseline and grind until Rybakina's UEs accumulate. Rybakina's IW p_srv=0.620 is below her true level. The 2024 Cincinnati final blueprint: Svitolina extends, Rybakina misfires. Market anchored on Rybakina's ranking and serve speed without adjusting for surface or Svitolina's 2026 form.

SCOUT+SIM: Svitolina +1.5 78% true vs 54c (+24pp, gate ~12% cleared decisively). PLAYSTYLE: Even in a losing scenario, Svitolina winning a set is near-structural. Her defensive baseline game forces 3-set outcomes — she doesn't blow off the court. IW slow courts (CPI 37) blunt Rybakina's flat serve, meaning Svitolina gets time to set up her counterpunching patterns on every ball. Rybakina's serve is her primary weapon; when it underperforms (as it has at IW 2026, p_srv=0.620 below career), tiebreak variance alone hands Svitolina a set. She beat Swiatek in 3 sets last round — same pattern: extend, absorb, strike in the third. Sim says 82% true for set-win. Scout anchors more conservatively at 78%. Either way, 54c for 78%+ is the best risk-adjusted play on the board.

SCOUT+SIM: Under 21.5 62% true vs 52c (+10pp, gate cleared). PLAYSTYLE: When Moutet wins this match — Scout says 84% probability — he wins it fast. H2H 2-0 Moutet including 6-3 6-2 at this exact Phoenix venue last year. Basilashvili 1-8 on hard in 2026, got here via a walkover, his 49% 1stIn collapses further when a lefty returner like Moutet jumps on every second ball. Moutet's left-handed return game targets the backhand on both ad and deuce — Basilashvili's serve mechanics fall apart under that pressure (double fault rate spikes, short rally errors). Sim avg game count inflated by unlikely Basilashvili win scenarios; Scout conviction is Under: when Moutet dominates, it's 6-3 6-2 type (17-18 games), well inside 21.5.

SCOUT+SIM: Over 23.5 65-70% true vs 51c (+14-19pp, gate cleared comfortably). PLAYSTYLE: Two strong servers meeting in a QF = tiebreaks = long match. Giron fatigue is a secondary edge for Borges on ML, but not enough margin — the Over is the clean entry.

v2.5 SCOUT+QUANT+SIM: Seidel ML 61.2% P_final vs 41c (+20.2pp). SCOUT PRIMARY: Market has Stakusic at 62c — a ranking inversion (Seidel #83 WTA vs Stakusic #130). Miami Laykold ITF-4 surface (slower than most hard courts, upgraded from ITF-3) combined with 70% humidity makes balls sit up and slow down further. This conditions punishes weak second serves even harder: the ball doesn't skid through the court, it hangs in Seidel's strike zone. Pipeline: Scout 55% / QUANT 53.8% / Sim 98.8% (extreme serve gap). P_final 61.2% blended. Market anchored on Stakusic's higher recent exposure, not serve quality. SofaScore-verified serve data.
